markdownRemark.html:
I recently finished building version 2 of vidbg.js, a minimal JavaScript video background plugin. I use Babel and Webpack to be able to write the newest JavaScript, while making it backwards compatible for older browsers that I need to support.

Webpack has a functionality to export your code as a “library” or in my case, a plugin with a configurable variable name. For example, for vidbg.js I have something like this in my webpack.config.js

output: {
  path: path.resolve(__dirname, 'dist'),
  filename: 'vidbg.js',
  library: 'vidbg',
  libraryTarget: 'var',
},

Ideally, since the libraryTarget is set to var I should be able to access my script through the vidbg variable.

However, I noticed that instead of accessing the variable through vidbg, I had to access it through vidbg.default() because I was using ES6 modules and doing a default export, like so:

class vidbg{
  ...
}

export default vidbg

After searching literally everywhere to no avail, I finally found the answer buried in the Webpack docs. All you have to do is add libraryExport with a value of default to your webpack.confi.js.

The Solution

output: {
  path: path.resolve(__dirname, 'dist'),
  filename: 'vidbg.js',
  library: 'vidbg',
  libraryTarget: 'var',
  libraryExport: 'default' // tell Webpack to export the default export, not the entire namespace
},

This tells Webpack to export the library on the default export now, instead of exporting the whole namespace object.

I hope someone can find this useful, since it took me hours to fix this one simple issue 🤦‍

Cheers!
